description: Function has too high cyclomatic complexity. Keep functions small and focused — extract branches into helper functions or sub-components to stay below the complexity threshold.
Variable or parameter name is too short (less than 2 characters). Single-letter names like e, v, p, s, h, m, q are not allowed. Use descriptive names instead.
Found complex boolean return. Instead of `if (condition) { return true } return false`, simplify to `return Boolean(condition)` or `return condition`.
The void operator is not allowed. To fire-and-forget a promise, use `.catch(console.error)` or standard block statements `{ fn() }` instead of `void fn()`.
Detected the use of `alert`, `confirm` and `prompt`. Browser built-in dialogs are obtrusive; use custom UI modal components instead.
Avoid using the `` tag. Use the built-in `` component from Next.js to leverage automatic optimization, lazy loading, and sizing features.
Require template literals instead of string concatenation to improve code readability.
Found non-null assertions (!). Avoid using the non-null assertion operator, use optional chaining (?.) or defensive fallback values instead to prevent runtime crashes.
Syntax error. Ensure codebase files are compliant with standard JavaScript syntax without parser conflicts.
Found unnecessary fragments. Redundant React fragments containing only one child or empty fragments should be removed.
Error objects should be used as Promise rejection reasons to preserve stack traces.
Found invalid characters in markup. Escape literal JSX characters like >, }, ', ", etc. using their HTML entity equivalent or wrapping them in string subexpressions.
Avoid using `console` in code that runs on browser. Remove console.log calls or wrap them with standard loggers.
Prefer not to declare variables in global scope. Top-level variables should be properly scoped as module-level variables.
Found mutable exports. Use `const` instead of `let` or `var` for exported values to prevent external mutations.
Found unused variables in TypeScript code. Remove unused variables or import references to clean up code.
Validation of JSX maximum depth. JSX tree is too deeply nested. Extract inner sections into sub-components to reduce nesting depth below 4.
Avoid using an element's index as the `key` prop in React lists. Use stable, unique identifiers instead.
Detected usage of the `any` type. Use `unknown` or specify explicit TypeScript types to ensure type safety.
Useless template literal found. Replace simple backtick strings with standard quotes if they do not contain interpolation or multi-line text.
Documentation comments not found for functions and classes. Add JSDoc comments to document inputs, outputs, and purpose of public functions and classes.
